back: The ability of different objects to respond to the same message in different ways.
Bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit, hiding internal state.
A mechanism by which one class acquires the properties and behaviors of another class.

front: What is the Single Responsibility Principle?
back: A class should have only one reason to change — it should do exactly one well-scoped thing.
Providing a component's collaborators from the outside instead of having it construct them itself.
